data.description: When you cast a spell that forces creatures to make a saving throw, you can use a sorcery point to protect some or all of those creatures from the spell's force. If you do, each chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.

When cast spell has range feet
you a that a of 5 ar
greater, you can spend 1sorcery to the
range the
of spell.
When cast spell has range touch,
you a that a of you
can
1
spend sorcery to the of
spell feel.
When cast spell has duration 1 of minute you a lhat a ar longer. you can spend 1 sorcery point to double its duration, to maximum duration 24
When cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can spend sorcery points to give the target disadvantage on its first save against that spell.
Green When cast spell has casting of you a that a time 1action, Red you spend sorcery points change casting the can 2 time to bonus for casting.

You can use your action to touch a creature you can see within 30 feet of you and cause one of the following effects: restore 2d8 + 1d6 hit points, remove one condition from that creature (such as blinded, poisoned, or charmed), or force the target to make a saving throw of your choice (the DC equals 8 + your sorcery point expenditure + your Charisma modifier). On a failed save, a target takes half the hit point damage and is affected by the other effect.

Your innate magic comes from draconic magic mingled with the blood of your ancestors. Most often, this trace of descent back to a mighty sorcerer who made a bargain with a dragon who even claimed to be a god.
At levei, choose type dragon your one of as 1st you The is by
Additionally, when you cast a spell, you can spend 1 sorcery point to speak, read, and write Draconic. Whenever make Charisma check interacting with your dragons, proficiency is applies the.
